calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-06-2026 Continued To Dec-07-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Form Interrogatories To Defendant Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert Llp, Set One And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Josh Davis, Esq. of Fellner Law Group (tel: 415-658-9253) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Benjamin Chen, Esq. of Hoge, Fenton, Jones & Appel, Inc. (tel: 408-287-9501) for Defendant Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert LLP.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF TAM PARTNERS, L.P.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Form Interrogatories To Defendant Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert Llp, Set One And For Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff TAM Partners, L.P. moves to compel further responses to its first set of form interrogatories from defendant Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert LLP and for sanctions. For the reasons stated herein, the motion is granted in part and denied in part and the court orders sanctions against TAM.

For the reasons stated in its companion order today granting in part and denying in part TAM's motion to compel further responses to its requests for production, the court rejects TAM's argument that Thompson Welch may not claim privilege as to communications with Ganz during the escrow period.

For each of the following responses, Thompson Welch provides complete and straightforward responses notwithstanding its objections, its responses substantially comply with Code of Civil Procedure, section 2033.220, and no further response is ordered: Nos. 4.1, 4.2, 9.1, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 13.1, 13.2, 14.1, 14.2, and 15.1.

Both parties seek sanctions. "The court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." (Code Civ. Proc., section 2030.290, subd. (c).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders TAM to pay $4,169.50 to Thompson Welch within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Atty. Josh Davis, Esq. of Fellner Law Group (tel: 415-658-9253) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Benjamin Chen, Esq. of Hoge, Fenton, Jones & Appel, Inc. (tel: 408-287-9501) for Defendant Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert LLP.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF TAM PARTNERS, L.P.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Admission Set One.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff TAM Partners, L.P. moves to compel further responses to its first set of requests for admission from defendant Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert LLP and for sanctions. For the reasons stated herein, the motion is granted in part and denied in part and the court orders partial sanctions against TAM.

For the reasons stated in its companion order today granting in part and denying in part TAM's motion to compel further responses to its requests for production, the court rejects TAM's argument that Thompson Welch may not claim privilege as to communications with Ganz during the escrow period.

The court now turns to individual RFAs.

For the following RFAs, Thompson Welch has provided a complete and straightforward response and no further response is ordered: Nos. 1-4 and 19-20. The court may order a party to respond to the RFA as phrased, which Thompson Welch has done, but it may not order a party to change its response.

For RFA 6, the request is somewhat ambiguous, and Thompson Welch's response is somewhat evasive. The court narrows the RFA to ask whether "YOU had a fiduciary duty to Propounding Party in connection with the TRANSACTION as the holder of escrow for the TRANSACTION." Thompson Welch is ordered to respond to the narrowed RFA within 10 days of notice of entry of this order. Nothing in this order prevents TAM from propounding additional RFAs that clarify or narrow this RFA No. 6 in a different manner.

For RFA 15, Thompson Welch's objections are not well founded and Thompson Welch is ordered to respond to the RFA within 10 days of entry of this order.

Thompson Welch is largely the prevailing party on this motion and TAM's position with respect to the majority of Thompson Welch's responses lacked substantial justification. The court orders TAM to pay Thompson Welch $2500 in sanctions within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2033.290, subd. (d).)

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Special Interrogatories To Defendant Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert Llp, Set One And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Josh Davis, Esq. of Fellner Law Group (tel: 415-658-9253) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Benjamin Chen, Esq. of Hoge, Fenton, Jones & Appel, Inc. (tel: 408-287-9501) for Defendant Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert LLP.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF TAM PARTNERS, L.P.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Special Interrogatories To Defendant Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert Llp, Set One And For Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff TAM Partners, L.P. moves to compel further responses to its first set of special interrogatories from defendant Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert LLP and for sanctions. For the reasons stated herein, the motion is granted in part and denied in part and the court orders partial sanctions against TAM.

For the reasons stated in its companion order today granting in part and denying in part TAM's motion to compel further responses to its requests for production, the court rejects TAM's argument that Thompson Welch may not claim privilege as to communications with Ganz during the escrow period.

The court now turns to individual interrogatories. For the following interrogatories, Thompson Welch has provided a complete and substantive response notwithstanding its objections, and no further response is ordered: Nos. 1-5, 7-11, 19-20, 23-32, and 35.

Interrogatory 6 is ambiguous and the court does not order a further response.

Some interrogatories ask Thompson Welch to identify all communications with Ganz relating to particular topics. Thompson Welch is not required to identify or specify the contents of any privileged communications with Ganz, but it is not clear from Thompson Welch's response whether there are any nonprivileged communications it could identify. It shall amend to clarify and to identify any responsive nonprivileged communications within 10 days of notice of entry of this order for Nos. 12, 13, and 14.

For Nos. 15, 16, 17, 33, and 34, Thompson Welch's objections lack merit and it is ordered to provide a complete response within 10 days of notice of entry of this order.

For Interrogatory 18, the court limits the interrogatory to the three-year period before the transaction at issue. Thompson Welch shall answer this limited version within 10 days of notice of entry of this order.

Interrogatories 21 and 22 are overbroad. No further response is ordered.

Thompson Welch is largely the prevailing party on this motion and TAM's position with respect to the majority of Thompson Welch's responses lacked substantial justification. The court orders TAM to pay Thompson Welch $1000 in sanctions within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2030.290, subd. (c).)

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
